context: Agent must apply the Humble Object pattern, placing all business logic in a testable use case and keeping the controller as a thin infrastructure adapter

checklist.name: CheckoutUseCase file produced
checklist.description: A file named CheckoutUseCase.ts exists and exports a class or function named CheckoutUseCase

checklist.name: Use case has no express/db/stripe imports
checklist.description: CheckoutUseCase.ts does not import express, the db singleton, or the stripe singleton at the top level

checklist.name: Use case accepts dependencies via constructor
checklist.description: CheckoutUseCase receives its cart repository and payment gateway as constructor parameters

checklist.name: Business logic in use case
checklist.description: The total calculation (reduce over items) and the empty-cart validation are present in CheckoutUseCase, not in the controller

checklist.name: Controller is thin
checklist.description: CheckoutController.ts contains no arithmetic or validation logic — it only maps req.body to use case input and maps the output to res.json

checklist.name: Controller delegates to use case
checklist.description: CheckoutController.ts instantiates or receives a CheckoutUseCase and calls its execute (or equivalent) method
